[[CH05-P1090]] You have hit your ChatGPT usage limit (plus plan). Try again in ~0 min. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1100]] Master Frost’s room had come apart in seconds. The monitor above his bed shrieked, red strobes cutting across displays that should have been steady green, and the emergency lighting turned everything thin and unreal. Frost thrashed against the sheets, his spare frame bowing and twisting as if the bed were trying to hold down a storm. His hands clawed at the air, grasping for something Marnette could not see, and the sound that had pulled her from sleep seemed to still be trapped in the room, sharp as pain. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1110]] She needed help. Elara was the only person she knew who might understand why. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1120]] *** <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1130]] Over the next few days the thought stopped being a possibility and became an intention, then a plan. Marnette would contact Elara. She would find a way back to Residence 7B. She would get into the Archive and find what Frost had pointed her towards, whatever it cost her to see it clearly. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1140]] What came after remained unclear, and the uncertainty frightened her more than the plan itself. If she found proof that salve lifespans were engineered, that the entire social order rested on biological manipulation and religious lies, what would she do with it. Who could she tell. How could she make anyone believe her. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1150]] Tell them, Frost had whispered at the end. Marnette still did not know who he meant. Other salves, perhaps. Yet would they even want the truth if it stripped away the one story that made their lives bearable. Would they believe her, or close their hands over their ears and call her dangerous. Would they respond like Lena, all warmth and worry, and then a quiet insistence that she rest and return to sense. Or like Thomas, eyes bright with need, ready to reach for an answer no matter what it burned. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1160]] The questions had no clean answers, but the first step was plain. She had to know. She had to see for herself what Frost’s Archive contained, not in fragments and half-glimpses but whole. Whatever came after would have to be built on that. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1170]] Six weeks after Frost’s death, Marnette woke before dawn with a clarity she had not felt since leaving his residence. Grey artificial morning leaked through the small window, revealing a room she had lived in for years and no longer recognised as home. Her prayer beads sat untouched on the bedside table. The carving of Elom faced her, familiar in shape and suddenly strange, its gaze no longer a comfort she could rely on. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1180]] She rose, pulled on her grey tunic, and studied herself in the small mirror by the door. The face looking back had not changed. Same dark eyes, same strong features, the same practiced calm that had carried her into Frost’s residence. The difference lay underneath, in the way her gaze held without softening. Something had shifted behind it, a hairline break that had also become an opening. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1190]] "Today," she said to her reflection. "I’ll find a way." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1200]] She did not know how. She did not know what obstacles waited, or what danger a single misstep might summon. She only knew she could not keep living like this. She could not keep performing faith that no longer held. She could not keep shaping her mouth around prayers that tasted false, then call it devotion. Frost’s promise sat in her like a stone. If she did not try, she would be breaking it every day. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1210]] Frost’s Archive waited in Residence 7B, holding secrets that could unmake the world she knew. Marnette Croft had spent her life practising acceptance until it felt like virtue. Now she was ready to ask what she had been trained never to ask, even if the questions tore her apart. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1220]] She took her prayer beads from the bedside table and let their familiar weight settle into her palm. For a moment she considered leaving them behind, a clean gesture, an easy symbol. The thought felt dishonest. Instead she tucked them beneath her collar, where they had always rested. They were part of her, whatever she believed now, and she could not cut them away without cutting herself. Perhaps the truth, when she found it, would tell her what the beads were for. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1230]] The corridor outside her quarters lay empty in the early light, flat and shadowless. Marnette headed for the transit platform with her steps kept even, as if steadiness were something she could choose and hold. Her mind had stopped circling and narrowed into purpose. She would get back to Residence 7B. She would get into the Archive. She would uncover whatever Frost had been trying to place in her hands. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1240]] Whatever followed, whatever consequences came with knowing what had been buried for centuries, she would meet them. The alternative was to spend the rest of her designated time performing ignorance, pretending the cracks in her faith were not there, then die without ever understanding why. She could not accept that any longer. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1250]] The cabin’s vibration rose under her feet as Marnette stepped aboard and took her seat, hands clasped to keep them from shaking. The route ahead was familiar. What it now meant was not. Behind her, the salve district kept to its morning routines, corridors filling, shifts beginning, prayers spoken on time, as if certainty were still available. Marnette stared at the closed doors and felt the threshold settle into place. The questions waiting for her had no safe answers, and the truths behind them would not be gentle. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1260]] Marnette watched the grey corridors fall away into the transit tubes, her familiar world sliding past the grimy window in clipped, repeating segments. Something moved under her ribs, too sharp to name cleanly. It might have been fear. It might have been hope. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1270]] Frost’s last words returned, stripped down to their bones. The Archive. Find the truth. They lied. The founder’s name, not the one she had been taught. Forgive. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1280]] She leaned closer to the window and whispered to the empty car, as if the promise could travel ahead of her. "I’m coming. I’ll find it. I promise." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1290]] Red dust lay beyond the dome, patient as ever, indifferent to vows and mourning. Somewhere in Residence 7B, behind polished floors and locked doors, a room full of records waited in silence. Frost had called it an Archive. Marnette felt it ahead of her like gravity, dangerous and unavoidable, asking to be opened. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH05-P1300]] Marnette Croft was finally ready to look. <!-- REV2 -->
